docs.title: README
docs.text:
This project aims to document the Scratch API and other features, such as the sb3 file format. The source of these docs is available on GitHub.

You can make contributions by opening a pull request on GitHub

The docs are licensed under MIT

docs.title: API
docs.text:
API stands for application-programmer-interface. Essentially what this means is a way to integrate your code with scratch.

Scratch itself provides a number of urls which can be used for interaction via web requests, shown below.

Info

It is recommended to use an existing library to interact with scratch instead of making your own

docs.title: Web API
docs.text:
In order to make requests to scratch, there are a multitude of different urls where you can make GET, POST, PUT, DELETE and other types of request.

These urls can be generally categorized by their subdomain and/or path.

Warning

This is not a complete list.

docs.title: Scratch
docs.text: URL Purpose https://scratch.mit.edu/ The main website https://api.scratch.mit.edu/ The main API for public use, documented on the scratch wiki https://assets.scratch.mit.edu/ Interaction with assets (costumes and sounds) https://backpack.scratch.mit.edu/ Interaction with the backpack https://scratch.mit.edu/site-api/ An API used internally by the scratch website. Provides access for the same things, but less restrictive, e.g. classroom alerts wss://clouddata.scratch.mit.edu/ This is where the scratch cloud server websocket lives https://projects.scratch.mit.edu Downloading projects https://uploads.scratch.mit.edu Thumbnails/profile images https://translate-service.scratch.mit.edu/ Translation requests - which the translate extension uses https://cdn.scratch.mit.edu Content delivery network - hosts images, css, js, emojis, etc. https://synthesis-service.scratch.mit.edu Text2Speech requests - which the Text2Speech extension uses

docs.title: Ocular
docs.text: URL Purpose https://jeffalo.net/api/ Jeffalo's API https://my-ocular.jeffalo.net/api/ API for ocular

docs.title: TurboWarp
docs.text: URL Purpose

docs.title: Existing libraries
docs.text:
There are a number of pre-existing libraries for interacting with the scratch API

Warning

This is not a complete list

PythonTypeScript Deprecated libraries:
  • scratchcloud
  • scratchclient
  • node-scratch-client
  • scratch-api (js)

docs.title: ScratchAttach
docs.text: A sync (developing async) python library for the scratch API and other site features.

docs.title: Scapi
docs.text: An async python library for the scratch web API, using aiohttp. Docs in Japanese.

docs.title: ScratchAttach.ts
docs.text: An async TypeScript library for the scratch API (missing features)

docs.title: Scratch CLI
docs.text:
Warning

This documentation currently does not apply. It is currently documenting a specification for the tool.

The Scratch CLI is a command line client for scratch that wraps scratchattach.

docs.title: Why Scratch CLI?
docs.text:
Scratch CLI is built to be good for account management. It makes it easy to:

  • Switch between accounts
  • Perform actions with multiple accounts
  • Manage certain groups of accounts

For more info, go here

docs.title: Sessions
docs.text:
A session represents a single 'log in' for a scratch account.

The session object: 

{
  "username": "faretek1",
  "id": "..."
}

docs.title: scratch login
docs.text: You will be automatically exited from the group. To stay in, use -G. Login to a scratch account and add it to the current group. You will be prompted for a username and password

docs.title: scratch login --sessid
docs.text: You will be automatically exited from the group. To stay in, use -G. Login to a scratch account and add it to the current group. You will be prompted for a session ID.

docs.title: Account groups
docs.text:
The CLI allows you to configure account groups. These are collections of 1+ accounts which you can perform actions with.

The group object: 

{
  "name": "FA",
  "sessions": [
    {
      "username": "faretek1",
      "id": "..."
    },
    {
      "username": "faretek",
      "id": "..."
    }
  ]
}

These are stored in the cookies as values for an object, not a list. The key is the name but lowercase.

docs.title: scratch ungroup
docs.text: This exits the current group and into the 'global' group. If you login now, it will make a new group with only 1 member, which you will automatically enter.

docs.title: scratch group
docs.text: Get the members of the group.

docs.title: scratch group rename
docs.text: Try to rename the group, if possible.

docs.title: scratch group delete
docs.text: Delete the group.

docs.title: scratch group
docs.text: List all groups
